items.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
items.authors: Jesse Bettencourt, Xindi Wu, Matan Atzmon et al.
items.abstract: Pretrained diffusion models serve as frozen teachers feeding downstream pipelines such as text-to-3D, single-step distillation, and data attribution. The teacher gradients these pipelines consume are Monte Carlo (MC) expectations over noise levels and Gaussian noise samples; their estimator variance...

items.title: Quantifying Hyperparameter Transfer and the Importance of Embedding Layer Learning Rate
items.abstract: Hyperparameter transfer allows extrapolating optimal optimization hyperparameters from small to large scales, making it critical for training large language models (LLMs). This is done either by fitting a scaling law to the hyperparameters or by a judicious choice of parameterization, such as Maxima...

items.title: AiraXiv: An AI-Driven Open-Access Platform for Human and AI Scientists
items.authors: Junshu Pan, Panzhong Lu, Yixuan Weng et al.
items.abstract: Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI) have accelerated the growth of both human-authored and AI-generated research outputs, placing increasing strain on traditional academic publishing systems and challenging the scalability of conference- and journal-centered paradigms amid rising submiss...

items.title: You Only Need Minimal RLVR Training: Extrapolating LLMs via Rank-1 Trajectories
items.authors: Zhepei Wei, Xinyu Zhu, Wei-Lin Chen et al.
items.abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a dominant paradigm for improving reasoning in large language models (LLMs), yet the underlying geometry of the resulting parameter trajectories remains underexplored. In this work, we demonstrate that RLVR weight trajectories are extr...

items.title: Uni-Edit: Intelligent Editing Is A General Task For Unified Model Tuning
items.authors: Dian Zheng, Manyuan Zhang, Hongyu Li et al.
items.abstract: Currently, enhancing Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) with image understanding, generation, and editing capabilities mainly relies on mixed multi-task training. Due to inherent task conflicts, such strategy requires complex multi-stage pipelines, massive data mixing, and balancing tricks, merely res...

items.title: Equilibrium Reasoners: Learning Attractors Enables Scalable Reasoning
items.authors: Benhao Huang, Zhengyang Geng, Zico Kolter
items.abstract: Scaling test-time compute by iteratively updating a latent state has emerged as a powerful paradigm for reasoning. Yet the internal mechanisms that enable these iterative models to generalize beyond memorized patterns remain unclear. We hypothesize that generalizable reasoning arises from learning t...

items.title: HITL-D: Human In The Loop Diffusion Assisted Shared Control
items.authors: Riley Zilka, Sergey Khlynovskiy, Allie Wang et al.
items.abstract: Autonomous manipulation systems have achieved remarkable capabilities, yet the integration of human expertise with diffusion-based policies in shared control remains relatively unexplored. In this paper, we propose Human-In-The-Loop Diffusion (HITL-D), a shared control framework that enhances user p...

items.title: Lost in Fog: Sensor Perturbations Expose Reasoning Fragility in Driving VLAs
items.abstract: Interpretable autonomous driving planners depend not only on generating explanations, but also on those explanations remaining reliable under real-world sensor degradation. In this paper we present a controlled perturbation study of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) robustness in autonomous driving, eval...
